finalSolution.contribution:
Life Support Contribution: Closed-loop ECLSS (Environmental Control and Life Support System) utilizing:
- Sabatier reactor for CO₂ → O₂ conversion
- Multi-stage water recycling (vapor compression distillation + reverse osmosis)
- Hydroponic algae bioreactors for supplemental O₂ and food production
- Radiation-shielded habitat modules with 0.5m regolith covering
- Redundant pressure regulation with 3 independent airlocks

**Energy Contribution:**
- Primary power generation via 10 MW fission reactor (Kilopower-derived), providing continuous baseload power independent of solar availability
- Redundant solar photovoltaic array (2 MW peak) for daytime supplemental power and emergency backup
- Regenerative fuel cell system (H₂/O₂) for energy storage, with 48-hour autonomy capacity
- High-temperature electrolysis for in-situ propellant production (O₂ from CO₂) as secondary output
- Thermal management system using regolith-based heat sinks and radiator arrays

NO_CHANGE

**Structure Contribution:**

- Modular habitat design using in-situ regolith-based 3D-printed structural shells (compressive strength >20 MPa) with interlocking pressure vessel geometry
- Buried habitat layout (2–3m regolith overburden) for passive radiation shielding and micrometeorite protection
- Inflatable tensile membrane inner liners for rapid deployment and pressure retention, supported by rigid external lattice
- Redundant structural segmentation: 6 interconnected domes with blast-proof bulkhead doors for compartmentalized failure isolation
- Foundation anchored via regolith-sintered piles to mitigate seismic and thermal cycling stresses
- Dust mitigation: electrostatic repulsion surfaces on all external structural elements and airlock vestibules

SIGNAL_STABILIZATION
